meta_description: Tahnee Hopman is an editor working across editorial strategy, structural editing, copyediting and publication production for organisations whose content needs to hold up under close reading.
hero_lead: I edit the writing that has to hold up: board reports, investor analysis, expert publications, and the magazines and campaigns that carry an organisation's name. Eighteen years in, and I still can't walk past a misplaced hyphen. That's maybe half the job. The other half is knowing which four hundred words of a chief executive's three thousand actually make an argument, and having the nerve to cut the rest.
intro_heading: Two kinds of editing, one set of instincts
intro_body:
Most of my career has run on two tracks that turn out to be the same skill. One is leading editorial strategy for organisations that need their content to sound like them, consistently, across a magazine, a website, a podcast and a stakeholder who wants everything in by Friday. The other is quieter and more forensic: editing technical and analytical reports for readers who will notice if a number in the executive summary doesn't match the number in table four. Both come down to the same question: does this actually say what you meant it to say, to the people who are going to read it closely?

I've spent the past few years doing both at once, and I've stopped thinking of them as separate disciplines. They're the same editorial instinct, just working at different distances from the page.
practice_heading: What that looks like in practice

pillars.heading: Strategy & direction
pillars.body: Setting the editorial rationale for a publication, then planning it in cycles that match how the business runs — budget season, results season — not a borrowed news calendar.

pillars.heading: Structure & substance
pillars.body: Turning scattered, high-conviction thinking into an argument that holds together, and giving the point a writer already made — usually buried in paragraph four — the position it deserves.

pillars.heading: Clarity, consistency & style
pillars.body: Line-level editing that keeps a writer's voice, plus the checks that catch what readers never notice unless it's wrong: a number, date or acronym out of step with itself.

pillars.heading: Production & proof
pillars.body: Staying involved through layout and final proof, in InDesign if needed, where restraint matters as much as attention and an unnecessary late change is the riskiest kind.

selected_work_intro: A short version. The longer one is [here](selected-work.html).

selected_work_items.title: INTHEBLACK
selected_work_items.body: Led editorial strategy for CPA Australia's flagship business magazine and its companion digital, podcast and video content. Produced the mental health and wellbeing special edition, winner of a Mumbrella Publish Award.

selected_work_items.title: Mufti & the ANZAC Appeal
selected_work_items.body: Ran the member magazine through a redesign, launched the organisation's first podcast, and grew online ANZAC Appeal donations 35% year on year, shortlisted for a Fundraising Excellence Award.

selected_work_items.title: EnergyQuarterly and industry reports
selected_work_items.body: Technical and analytical editing for quarterly reports on the Australian energy sector, read closely by industry leaders, investors and government.
